banner.title: Vision Beyond Pixels
banner.description:
Our service goes beyond video analysis 
to create real value. 

With AI-powered technology, we deliver 
instant solutions for theft prevention, 
industrial safety, and marketing optimization. 

Unlike conventional services that take about 
two weeks to analyze, our patented technology 
compresses video by up to 97%, 
enabling real-time response.

problem.title:
The Challenge
 We Address
problem.description:
As offline stores regain attention and experiential spaces expand, theft has become a growing concern.

Traditional RFID tags and video surveillance solutions often face high costs and frequent malfunctions, limiting their effectiveness.

To address this, we developed a patented real-time AI video analysis technology.

By applying neural network–based video compression, we process high-resolution data efficiently and deliver real-time insights into customer flow, interests, and behavior patterns.

1.title: Retail Analytics
1.description: Analyze customer movement, dwell time, and product interests to optimize store operations and boost sales.

2.title: Retail Security
2.description: Prevent theft using RFID tags, CCTV, and security gates, with real-time detection of suspicious activity.

3.title: Smart Factory Safety
3.description: Detect and respond to risks in factories using sensors and AI.

4.title: Parking Control System
4.description: Smart parking management with license plate recognition and barriers.

5.title: Marketing Optimization
5.description: Utilize real-time insights to enhance customer engagement and improve marketing strategies.

section1.title:
Beyond the pixels, 
we see the bigger 
world
section1.description:
Bigtablet goes beyond simple AI video analysis. 
We enhance the value of our clients and further 
support their sustainable growth through effective 
marketing strategies.

section2.title:
Endless reflection 
and thought elevate 
our value
section2.description:
At Bigtablet, our team constantly reflects, questions, and refines.
Through this process, we strive to provide clients and users with more convenient and
 tailored analytics services.
We go beyond simple data collection and analysis, moving forward each day in pursuit of the deeper value that lies beyond.

team.title: The People of Bigtablet
team.description: Introducing the team shaping the future of bigtablet together.

jiho.name: Jiho Moon
jiho.description: An adventurer in pursuit of optimal efficiency

qa1.q: Hello, Mr. Jiho Moon! Could you please give us a brief self-introduction?
qa1.a: My name is Jiho Moon, the CEO of Bigtablet. I began my professional career a bit earlier than most, driven by my curiosity and eagerness to explore various fields. Once I’m interested in something, I study and learn until I truly understand it. Looking back, my early start in the professional world, combined with this curiosity-driven mindset, has shaped who I am today. It’s also what has allowed me to work with Bigtablet for the past three years without giving up until problems were solved.

qa2.q: Could you briefly describe your current responsibilities at Bigtablet?
qa2.a: In many ways, I oversee nearly everything at Bigtablet from start to finish. My main responsibilities focus on defining the company’s direction, ensuring sustainable growth, and setting strategies to reach our future milestones.

qa3.q: What inspired you to start your own company?
qa3.a: Many people think entrepreneurship is only for experts in a specific field or those with a revolutionary idea. For me, it was more about identifying problems and finding fulfillment in solving them. Seeing the company grow as a result of our collective efforts has been deeply rewarding. That’s why I no longer see Bigtablet as just a company I founded in college, but as a true partner in solving real-world challenges.

qa4.q: What kind of company would you like Bigtablet to become in the future?
qa4.a: My goal is to grow Bigtablet from a startup into a global AI solutions company. Together with our talented team members, I want to build upon our strong company culture and establish Bigtablet as an influential, specialized leader in the global market.

qa5.q: What do you think is the biggest advantage of working at Bigtablet?
qa5.a: I believe the greatest advantage is the opportunity to grow together in a fast-evolving organization. It’s not only about professional growth — it’s about the synergy created when people from different backgrounds come together, share knowledge, and learn from each other. Of course, our employee benefits are also quite nice!

qa6.q: Lastly, do you have any words for your current and future team members?
qa6.a: Bigtablet is currently on a steep growth trajectory. With rapid growth often come challenges and areas for improvement, but I want to express my sincere gratitude to everyone who has been part of this journey. To future team members, I look forward to working with passionate individuals who want to make history together in the AI industry. Thank you!

minho.name: Minho Park
minho.description: An engineer driven to change the world through technology

qa1.q: Hello Minho! Could you please introduce yourself briefly?
qa1.a: Hello, I’m Minho Park, currently leading research and development at BigTablet.

qa2.q: The title of CTO might sound unfamiliar to some. Could you tell us what it means and what your role involves?
qa2.a: CTO stands for Chief Technology Officer — the person responsible for overseeing a company’s overall technology strategy and execution. At BigTablet, I lead the development of our AI CCTV solutions and various R&D initiatives, managing both internal and external collaborative projects. I’m also in charge of technical administration such as preparing R&D proposals, writing reports, and handling intellectual property. In essence, a CTO bridges technology and business strategy to drive company growth.

qa3.q: Could you tell us a bit about what you’re currently developing at BigTablet?
qa3.a: We’re developing a platform for video AI analytics based on VCM — Video Coding for Machines. Traditional video compression focuses on what’s visually pleasing to humans, while we’re building compression optimized for AI interpretation. Our system preserves high resolution for objects of interest while reducing unnecessary background data, redundant frames, and excessive color information. This allows us to cut video size by up to 70% compared to standard codecs, while maintaining object detection and tracking accuracy. With this technology, we’re expanding into fields like smart retail and smart city solutions.

qa4.q: It must have been challenging to develop products in the early stages. What difficulties did you face?
qa4.a: Coming from a research background, joining BigTablet was my first hands-on experience in real product development. Unlike academic research, we had to design for user interface and experience, which turned out to be quite challenging. While improving algorithm performance was familiar to me, making it intuitive and usable for real users required many iterations. In the early days, limited resources and manpower meant I had to juggle development, operation, and testing at once. Prioritization and efficiency became critical. Through this, I learned to think beyond technical perfection — considering productization and market fit together.

qa5.q: Lastly, what kind of technological vision is BigTablet pursuing?
qa5.a: Our vision is captured in the phrase ‘Vision Beyond Pixels.’ We aim to go beyond mere image recognition — to interpret context and meaning beyond pixels, creating new value through technology.

qa1.q: Hello Chaehyung! Could you please introduce yourself briefly?
qa1.a: Hello, I’m Chaehyung Lee, and I’m in charge of administrative affairs at Bigtablet Inc.

qa2.q: Could you briefly introduce your role?
qa2.a:
At Bigtablet, I’m mainly responsible for operational tasks related to our ongoing projects, as well as sales and customer service support.
Since we don’t yet have a dedicated department for areas like accounting, my work often involves handling a variety of administrative tasks as needed.

In the future, I expect to focus more on customer service operations as our services continue to expand and evolve.

qa3.q: Was there a particular reason or moment that led you to join Bigtablet?
qa3.a: There wasn’t really a grand reason or turning point. Before graduation, I was looking for companies to do my field training at, and Bigtablet happened to be close by. But what really drew me was that the CEO was young — I thought other places might feel too hierarchical or rigid. So I joined for my field training, and that connection eventually led me to stay on as an intern, and then as a full-time employee.

qa4.q: Have there been any moments when you felt, 'Ah, I’m really doing well at my job'?
qa4.a: Rather than feeling like I’m doing particularly well, I tend to feel proud whenever the company achieves progress — like when we secure new support projects or take meaningful steps forward as a team. It’s less of a self-congratulatory feeling and more of a reminder that there’s still a lot more I want to do, a kind of mixed emotion between satisfaction and motivation.

qa5.q: How would you like to grow or what kind of role do you hope to take on in the company moving forward?
qa5.a: Most people imagine climbing to the top of a company as their goal, but that’s not really me. My motto is ‘steady and long.’ I once thought about how I’d like to be like a fan — not as powerful as an air conditioner, but still something that quietly helps others. It’s a bit of a metaphor, but I want to be someone who’s helpful and reliable, even if not always in the spotlight. (Of course, that doesn’t mean I plan to stop working hard after one season!)

qa6.q: What does Bigtablet mean to you personally?
qa6.a: At first, I thought it was a slow train, but it turned out to be an express one. Still, if I had to describe Bigtablet, I’d say it’s a lot like the moon. Sometimes it feels so big and distant, yet it radiates this quiet beauty, like a soft halo in the night sky. It’s ever-changing and full of life. Like watching the moon through a high-speed camera — even when it’s momentarily hidden, it remains the brightest, most noticeable thing in the sky. Maybe ‘the North Star’ would be a more fitting comparison, but somehow, ‘the moon’ feels closer to my heart — because it’s something I hope will keep growing and shining brighter.

sungho.name: Sungho Kim
sungho.description: A designer who listens to the voice of users

qa1.q: Hello Sungho! Could you please introduce yourself briefly?
qa1.a: I'm Sungho Kim, a product designer with broad experience in planning and design within the B2B domain. I enjoy listening to users, communicating with teams, and building the foundation of services.

qa2.q: Could you tell us about your role at Bigtablet?
qa2.a: I organize requirements through user research and serve as a bridge between C-Level executives and the development team. In particular, I'm responsible for rapidly designing MVP models to validate market response, then incrementally refining the service based on those insights.

qa3.q: What motivated you to join Bigtablet?
qa3.a: After experiencing the dramatic efficiency gains from integrating AI into my workflow, I wanted to directly design AI solutions that enhance users' productivity. I also wanted to grow within a healthy organizational culture alongside proactive, young colleagues — which led me to join the team.

qa4.q: Could you share your design philosophy or the direction you pursue?
qa4.a: My design philosophy begins with the Bauhaus principle of 'practical essence,' passes through Dieter Rams' 'honest restraint,' and converges on the human-centered systems perfected by Apple and Google. I believe design should be an intelligent order that solves user problems beyond mere appearance. Stripping away unnecessary decoration and cognitive noise so that function itself becomes an intuitive language — distilling complex technology into the simplest, purest user experience — is the essence of the modern product design I pursue.

qa5.q: What goals do you hope to achieve at Bigtablet?
qa5.a: My goal is to design optimal experiences that allow AI technology to seamlessly integrate into users' workflows. Beyond personal growth, I want to contribute to the success of projects that innovate how people work — together with great colleagues.

gyeongmin.name: Gyeongmin Kim
gyeongmin.description: Be it. A PM who make dreams come true

qa1.q: Hello, Kyungmin! Could you briefly introduce yourself?
qa1.a: Hello, I’m Kyungmin Kim, currently working as a Product Manager at Bigtablet.

qa2.q: Could you tell us about your role at Bigtablet?
qa2.a: I am responsible for turning product visions into reality. My role involves defining what to build, why it matters, and how it should be executed by connecting customer needs with business goals. I lead the overall process from planning to development, setting priorities and ensuring smooth collaboration across teams.

qa3.q: What motivated you to join Bigtablet?
qa3.a: As AI technology rapidly expands across industries, I wanted to contribute directly to its growth. Bigtablet’s AI video analytics technology, in particular, addresses critical industrial challenges such as safety, efficiency, and productivity. I was confident that this field could drive meaningful industrial change, which led me to join the company.

qa4.q: You previously worked as a developer. What made you transition into a PM role?
qa4.a: While working as a developer, I experienced the entire process from planning to deployment and realized that projects without clear direction and goals often struggle. Even the best code loses value if the ‘why’ behind it is unclear. This led me to pursue the PM role, where I could define customer problems first and connect them strategically to business objectives.

qa5.q: What goals do you hope to achieve at Bigtablet?
qa5.a: My goal is to transform abstract AI visions into concrete products that succeed in the market. By leading innovation through AI video analytics, I want to enhance customer safety and operational efficiency. Ultimately, I strive to be a PM who doesn’t just envision ideas, but turns them into reality and contributes meaningfully to Bigtablet’s growth.

yerim.name: Yerim Kwon
yerim.description: A product manager who sees the beauty in dreams

qa1.q: Hello Yerim! Could you please introduce yourself briefly?
qa1.a: Hi! I’m Yerim Kwon, currently working as a Product Manager at BigTablet.

qa2.q: Could you tell us a bit about your role at BigTablet?
qa2.a: As a PM, I’m responsible for performance management and supporting our development team. My main focus is to help developers stay fully immersed in their work by taking care of everything around them.

qa3.q: The title ‘PM’ might sound unfamiliar to some. What exactly does a Product Manager do?
qa3.a: PM can mean many things, but in my case, it stands for Product Manager. A product manager is someone who constantly asks ‘why’—supporting the process of building better products that customers truly want. We connect different teams and ensure clear communication throughout development. In IT, that means coordinating with departments like planning, design, and development to define features and balance what customers want with what’s technically possible.

qa4.q: I heard you graduated from a Meister high school. What made you decide to pursue a career in IT and become a PM?
qa4.a: Ever since I was young, I dreamed of entering society early. My motto has always been ‘walk a different path.’ So instead of going to college, I wanted to gain real-world experience as soon as possible. I’d always been interested in computers, so naturally I wanted to make that my profession. During my time at Meister High School, I studied web development for two years, but I realized that I truly enjoyed interacting with people and supporting projects across all areas. That’s when I decided to pursue the PM role.

qa5.q: What challenges have you faced while working as a PM?
qa5.a: There are many challenges, but automation is probably the hardest part. Automation is meant to make things more efficient, but sometimes the process itself feels inefficient or overly complicated. Still, once it’s properly set up, it can dramatically improve workflow. I often think deeply about how tasks should be structured so that my teammates can work more efficiently. That kind of problem-solving mindset has become a big part of how I approach my work.

qa6.q: Finally, what made you decide to join BigTablet, and what are your goals here?
qa6.a: Before joining BigTablet, I applied to several companies, but I often ran into obstacles like educational background and the tough job market. Since most PM roles require developer experience or internal transfers, it wasn’t easy for someone new like me. I wanted a freer, startup-style environment where I could experience everything from A to Z. So when BigTablet sent a job posting to my school, I was thrilled—it felt like destiny. I think the most valuable thing I want to achieve here is to grow together with the team. Startups evolve alongside their members, and that shared growth mirrors my own journey as a young professional. I want to keep learning and growing with BigTablet.

gunwoo.name: Gunwoo Kim
gunwoo.description: A developer who strives for constant growth

qa1.q: Hello Gunwoo! Could you please introduce yourself briefly?
qa1.a: Hello! I’m Gunwoo Kim, a backend developer at BigTablet. I joined the company in 2025 and have been working hard with our development team ever since. 😁

qa2.q: Could you tell us a bit about your role at BigTablet?
qa2.a: I’m mainly responsible for server development and infrastructure management. In short, I handle most of the backend operations except for frontend development.

qa3.q: You mentioned that you’re in charge of backend development — what exactly does a backend developer do?
qa3.a: Backend development involves working with the data and logic that users don’t see directly. I build and manage servers, databases, and deployment workflows. To explain it a bit more, backend developers handle everything that happens from the moment a user signs up — including logins, data communication, and even security — designing, developing, and maintaining the entire system that supports the service.

qa4.q: You and Yerim both graduated from a Meister high school. What made you decide to pursue a career in development?
qa4.a: I’ve always been interested in IT, and since many people around me were developers, I naturally grew curious about the field. Initially, I studied to become an Android app developer, but after discovering backend development, I realized it suited me much better — and I really enjoyed it. So I shifted my focus to backend, which turned out to be one of the best decisions I’ve made. After entering Meister High School, I prepared to become a developer, and now I’m proudly part of BigTablet’s development team.

qa5.q: What are some of the pros and cons you’ve experienced while working on the development team?
qa5.a: Since I joined in the early development stage, there were times when the basic infrastructure was lacking. Building things from scratch and learning new technologies at the same time was quite challenging. Documentation, in particular, was tough for me — as a developer used to coding, creating templates and writing technical documents while developing was a new experience. But through discussions and collaboration with teammates, I’ve been improving both technically and personally. It’s rewarding to see progress every day and to feel like I’m putting together a big puzzle piece by piece.

qa6.q: Lastly, what are your personal goals at BigTablet?
qa6.a: My personal growth is important, but I believe it’s just as crucial for the team and the organization to grow together. I want to keep experimenting with and adopting new technologies, and someday become part of a leading big tech company.

sangmin.name: Sangmin Park
sangmin.description: A developer who never stops learning

qa1.q: Hello Sangmin! Could you please introduce yourself briefly?
qa1.a: Hello, I’m Sangmin Park, a frontend developer at BigTablet.

qa2.q: Could you tell us a bit about your role at BigTablet?
qa2.a: I’m responsible for visualizing the data analyzed from CCTV footage — one of BigTablet’s core functions — and presenting it to users in a clear and intuitive way.

qa3.q: You mentioned that you work on frontend development. What exactly does a frontend developer do?
qa3.a: In our company, I handle all web-related development. One of my main tasks is developing the service that visualizes analysis results from CCTV footage so users can easily understand the data through the web.

qa4.q: You, Yerim, and Gunwoo all graduated from a Meister high school. What inspired you to become a developer?
qa4.a: I first encountered coding during middle school in an information technology class using Scratch. I found the process of thinking through problems and seeing the results of my ideas incredibly rewarding — that sense of accomplishment made me want to pursue a career as a developer.

qa5.q: What’s been the most challenging part of your work so far, and what do you enjoy the most?
qa5.a: Since I’m currently the only frontend developer, there are times when the workload feels a bit heavy. However, I’m very grateful for the support I receive from the company and the culture we’ve built — where everyone trusts and respects each other’s work. That positive environment makes it enjoyable to work here.

qa6.q: Finally, what do you hope to achieve at BigTablet, and what kind of developer do you want to become?
qa6.a: My goal is to deliver BigTablet’s vision and services flawlessly. I find the projects we’re working on exciting and meaningful, so I want to build them without any issues. In the long run, I want to become a developer who can turn ideas into reality. While it’s easy to imagine things, bringing those ideas to life is the real challenge — and that’s the kind of developer I aspire to be.

byungjun.name: Park Byungjun
byungjun.description: A developer who cares about users

qa1.q: Hello Byungjun! Could you briefly introduce yourself?
qa1.a: Hello, I'm Park Byungjun, an app developer at Bigtablet.

qa2.q: Could you briefly explain the responsibilities you handle?
qa2.a: I develop and maintain the mobile applications required by the company.

qa3.q: You mentioned that you work on app development. Could you explain what exactly you do?
qa3.a: I build applications that run on smartphones. This includes designing screens, implementing features, integrating with servers, and managing updates or bug fixes to ensure the app runs smoothly.

qa4.q: What made you decide to join Bigtablet?
qa4.a: I have been developing Android and cross-platform apps for about three years since entering high school. Many of my previous projects were limited to small user groups or student-focused services. At Bigtablet, I can develop services tailored for real B2B and B2C clients and see the actual impact of my work, which strongly appealed to me.

qa5.q: As a graduate of a Meister High School, what led you to choose the path of becoming a developer?
qa5.a: I first became interested in development while creating Discord bots in the sixth grade. By the time I reached my third year of middle school, I felt that practical, hands-on education at a Meister High School would help me grow faster than a traditional university path. I genuinely enjoy writing code and seeing tangible results, which naturally led me to pursue development as a career.

qa6.q: What goals do you hope to achieve at Bigtablet?
qa6.a: My initial goal is to develop reliable applications that deliver real convenience to users. In the long term, I hope to grow into a team leader who can guide a development team and contribute to strengthening Bigtablet’s service capabilities.

cookie.message: This website uses cookies for a better experience.
